titleStmt.title: Countermanding order from Eoin MacNeill to regional commanders, 22 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Eoin MacNeill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: The handwritten note, embossed with MacNeill's (1867-1945) home address, was delivered to volunteers in the Kildare area.Eoin MacNeill was an Irish scholar, nationalist politician and founder of the Irish Volunteers. In 1916 MacNeill was led to believe, through a forged document, that a crackdown on the Volunteers by the British authorities was imminent. This prompted him to order a general mobilisation for the Easter weekend. When he learned the truth about the forgery, the seizure of German arms and arrest of Roger Casement, he sent messengers around the country ordering a general demobilisation, following this up with an advertisement in the Sunday Independent. He succeeded in delaying the Rising by one day and confining it largely to Dublin and was subsequently arrested.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Woodtown Park, Rathfarnham, Co. Dublin. 22 April 1916 Volunteers completely
deceived. All orders
for tomorrow Sunday
are entirely cancelled. EÃ³in MacNeill
